scenario: A Form's schema (JSON definition) is what's stored / version-controlled in Web Modeler. The team wonders if there's a distinction between schema versions and deployed (published) versions of a Form.
question: What's the distinction between Form schema versions and deployed Form versions?

options.text: Form schema versions and deployed Form versions are the same thing with no meaningful distinction -- this conflates two separate version tracking systems. Web Modeler schema versions are development-side revisions of the Form's JSON definition that may include many intermediate drafts. Deployed versions are explicit cluster-side snapshots created only when a team actively deploys the form. Many schema saves may occur between deployments, and only deployed versions are visible to Tasklist for task rendering.

Camunda Forms have no versioning whatsoever -- this contradicts how both Web Modeler and the Zeebe cluster track form definitions. Web Modeler maintains a revision history for each form's JSON definition, allowing teams to compare changes, revert drafts, and track evolution. The cluster maintains deployed form definition versions. Both layers independently track their versions using different scopes: development history vs deployment snapshots.

Only the cluster side tracks versions of deployed Forms; Web Modeler has no version history for form schemas -- this understates Web Modeler's development-side capabilities. Web Modeler tracks schema version history for form definitions, enabling teams to see how a form evolved over time, compare revisions, and restore earlier states if needed. The cluster-side deployed versions are a separate, independently maintained version sequence triggered only by explicit deployment actions.

Schema versions are revisions of the Form's JSON definition stored / tracked in Web Modeler (each save / commit creates a new version in the project's history). Deployed versions are snapshots of the Form deployed to a cluster -- when a Form is deployed, the cluster's Tasklist sees that specific snapshot at that version. Multiple schema saves don't auto-deploy; explicit deploy creates a cluster-side Form definition version. Same model as BPMN: Web Modeler tracks drafts; deployment creates cluster-side versions.

a.text: Incorrect. Two distinct version concepts: Web Modeler schema versions (development-side): Each save / commit in Web Modeler creates a version in the project's history. Track drafts as the Form evolves: add a field, change validation, refine layout. Diff between versions; revert to a prior version; collaborate on changes. Not deployed to any cluster yet. Deployed Form versions (cluster-side): Triggered by explicit "Deploy" action (or programmatic API). Creates a Form definition version in the target Zeebe cluster. Tasklist resolves form references against deployed versions. Workflow: 1. Modeler iterates the Form in Web Modeler (creating schema versions). 2. When ready, modeler deploys → cluster receives Form definition v1. 3. Continued schema iteration → eventual redeploy → cluster receives v2. 4. Tasklist uses the appropriate cluster-side version based on the BPMN's form reference. Same model as BPMN / DMN: development versions (Web Modeler) vs deployment versions (cluster).
